title: Chapter 367 – Peerless Master (2)
content:
Silk robe hems brushed against the grass blades. A sound that stood out in the silence.

The rising talents had been examining the traces left by the Nine Sects' martial arts when they suddenly doubted their ears.

Even those who had been glaring at the Five Heroes of Passion Protection turned their heads.

"The Radiant Demon Wing Leader of Desolate Fortress...?"

"I saw Lightning Genius at the Hangzhou Martial Alliance conference, but that man has a different build."

"He was much younger."

"Is there no one better to tell such lies to than the two venerable elders of Qingcheng and Emei...?"

The group of rising talents accompanying the Nine Sects' masters.

All twelve had properly learned and mastered internal energy breathing.

This meant they possessed stable sensory cultivation. Incomparable to wandering swordsmen without even a decent sect.

They maintained their energy sense while attending to other matters. They had the skill for it, and had always needed to on the chaotic battlefields they had traversed.

Naturally, they were scrutinizing every move of the black-robed young man with their senses.

An uncomfortable atmosphere was inevitable.

The two venerable elders of the Nine Sects should not be disrespected. Disrespecting Divine Nun Zi Wei and Taoist Master Qing Yin would undermine the dignity of those positioned as the group's representatives.

"Fellow Taoists, please be patient. This benefactor's energy is extraordinary, so we shouldn't dismiss his words as mere falsehoods."

"I agree."

Those who would lead the Nine Sects in the future.

Golden Staff Divine Woman of Emei Sect and Crimson Cloud Dragon of Qingcheng Sect didn't react with anger at the black-robed young man's speech and conduct.

After verbally restraining their group, they studied Jeong Yeon-shin from behind their elders with deep, measured expressions.

"Radiant Demon Wing Leader?"

Taoist Master Qing Yin, with his back to Crimson Cloud Dragon, repeated Jeong Yeon-shin's words. Doubt filled his eyes.

"Can you take responsibility for those words?"

He had sensed a faint yet powerful energy wave when Jeong Yeon-shin deployed his movement technique earlier. His words and manner couldn't help but be cautious.

"Must I?"

Jeong Yeon-shin's head tilted slightly to the side. He thought: Is there any need to engage in lengthy conversation? It's not as though we'll be traveling together.

Sensing this attitude, the middle-aged Buddhist nun who had been crudely hammering the ground with her iron staff pressed her palms together and spoke.

"It is a karmic connection from another life. They say even brushing sleeves in passing is a bond, yet the benefactor seems to have no particular business with us."

"Our sleeves didn't brush."

Just as Jeong Yeon-shin replied with casual bluntness.

"The Dragon Phoenix Twin Poisons of the Tang family...? What is this!"

"There's no life energy! They've already passed away...!"

A commotion erupted from behind. The Sichuan rising talents who had been shepherded by the Nine Sects figures like nursemaids.

They had recognized the Tang siblings that Jeong Yeon-shin had laid down, even from a distance. Some, having cultivated eye techniques, noted the absence of life energy.

"I knew it! Some impostor pretending to be a Desolate Fortress martial artist!"

"He could genuinely be the Radiant Demon Wing Leader. It wouldn't be the first or last time the imperial sword has taken a martial artist's head!"

Footsteps rang out. The presences moved with extreme speed, apparently using lightness skill to retrieve the bodies of the Dragon Phoenix Twin Poisons.

Jeong Yeon-shin thought: This won't do.

With chaos descending upon the world, these people — who would crumble at a monkey's chopsticks — couldn't possibly escort the Dragon Phoenix Twin Poisons' bodies safely back to the Tang family.

"Stop."

He spoke without looking back.

The rising talents immediately responded.

"I don't know by what authority you speak this way, but even if what you just said is true, we cannot comply."

"Entrust direct descendants of the Eight Great Clans to a Desolate Fortress martial artist?"

"You should be the one questioned first. You might have killed them here yourself."

Those rushing toward the Tang siblings with large strides.

In truth, Divine Nun Zi Wei and Taoist Master Qing Yin, who had properly sensed Jeong Yeon-shin's martial prowess, merely remained in place, wary of him.

Golden Staff Divine Woman and Crimson Cloud Dragon, acclaimed as the finest rising talents, wore troubled expressions, caught between the rising talents and the two elders.

It was then.

Jeong Yeon-shin said nothing more.

He simply stepped forward with an indifferent face.

A slow movement, like taking a stroll. Was it an illusion? Faint starlight seemed to glimmer at his toes.

Behind the light trailed an afterimage. In an instant, Jeong Yeon-shin's form blurred and distorted. Simultaneously, every blade of undergrowth that had been leaning toward him bent its head in the opposite direction.

Whoosh!

The eyes of the four Nine Sects members widened.

The sound of air being gently displaced brushed their ears, and before it faded, they realized Jeong Yeon-shin had already passed them.

He was a master. And not just any master. Crimson Cloud Dragon whipped his head in the opposite direction. But the dull impact sounded faster than his gaze could follow.

The rising talents who had reached for the Tang siblings' bodies.

Among them, the first three were losing their balance and toppling, the scene slowly searing itself into his eyes.

And the black-robed young man gliding past them, scattering faint clusters of light.

With movements broken into segments like constellations, a faint white radiance crumbled in long streaks from Jeong Yeon-shin's fingertips, ephemeral as an illusion.

'When did he...?!'

It was a divine skill.

Dancing through space in an instant. A realm of entirely different caliber.

The energy waves rising from his hands scattered star-like radiance through the air, and as if some great spectacle had taken place, every rising talent who had surrounded the siblings was flung outward, rolling across the ground.

After that dull sound faded, silence descended.

Crimson Cloud Dragon couldn't even discern what form the young man's technique had taken.

The seemingly simple movements and palm techniques were strangely impossible to engrave in his mind.

He immediately understood why.

'Insane... primordial principles...!'

The trajectories contained an incredible density of subtleties.

Every muscle fiber's movement, even the fragments of internal energy that sparked into the air like embers, moved with deliberate intention. All in a single instant.

Crimson Cloud Dragon glanced to the side.

He saw Golden Staff Divine Woman with her lips slightly parted.

Her talent was no less than his own. She must have experienced the same crushing sense of distance in that moment.

'That level of martial prowess at that age. The former Peng family head? No, even she would struggle to match this. Besides the Young Sect Leader of Ming Cult, there's no one else...'

Little Sword Queen, Golden Staff Divine Woman of Emei, Crimson Cloud Dragon of Qingcheng.

The three had formed friendships in childhood, and all were spoken of as talents who would lead the next wave of the martial world — until they each hit a wall. Little Sword Queen when she encountered the Radiant Demon Wing Leader of Desolate Fortress. Golden Staff Divine Woman and Crimson Cloud Dragon in this very moment.

As gravity settled into the eyes of the two Nine Sects rising talents.

Swish.

Jeong Yeon-shin slowly turned. The Luminous Wheel energy that had been dwelling in his hands vanished completely.

"This is a divine sword crafted by the Dragon Phoenix Twin Poisons."

He placed his hand on the silver sword hilt at his waist.

"Do not interfere. My debt of gratitude comes first."

The gazes of the Nine Sects martial artists, including those rising talents who hadn't fainted, briefly dropped to the sword hilt. Jeong Yeon-shin paid no attention and continued.

"Where is this place?"

"...The Stone Tranquil Road of Deyang."

Divine Nun Zi Wei, who had been staring at him, answered abruptly.

The master-aunt of Golden Staff Divine Woman. After briefly scanning the iron staff she held, Jeong Yeon-shin inwardly shook his head — but thought it fortunate.

If this was Deyang, it wasn't far from the Tang family.

There should be a fairly large village down the road. He could buy coffins and a cart there to transport the bodies.

He couldn't continue carrying the siblings' bodies. It wasn't proper respect for the noble dead.

"To the Tang family...?"

Taoist Master Qing Yin asked.

Jeong Yeon-shin gave a rough nod, then carefully lifted the siblings and tucked them beneath each arm.

The rising talents sprawled on the ground watched the scene in blank silence.

Thud.

As he turned and began to walk, the sunset light fell upon the "Desolate" character on his shoulder.

Golden Staff Divine Woman and Crimson Cloud Dragon, watching Jeong Yeon-shin's retreating back from a distance, spoke.

"Let's follow him."

"We should verify, shouldn't we?"

The middle-aged elders sensed something in the two voices — a mixture of divergence and passion.

The competitive fire that martial artists of the same age harbor toward their peers. Divine Nun Zi Wei and Taoist Master Qing Yin silently nodded.

They were sect elders.

They couldn't fault their disciples for harboring unreasonable competitive spirit.

They needed to visit the Tang family regardless. Something major had happened.

***

***

A manor with high walls stretching endlessly in both directions.

Beneath the sunlight pouring down on the fortress-like walls that symbolized rare power and wealth in Sichuan, thunderous sounds echoed without cease.

With each heavy impact, the ground heaved and rose.

The Tang family.

The home of Sichuan's foremost noble clan had long since become a battlefield, consumed by faction warfare.

"That area is empty!"

"Prepare for poison techniques! Don't just focus on lightness skill — raise your protective energy to the extreme...!"

"The flank is breached! Block it!"

Countless martial artists were hurtling in every direction. Forms too numerous to count collided and fell in rapid succession.

Amid this chaos, the energy waves that occasionally clashed with tremendous force burst into heavy vibrations, and beneath them, fragments of shattered roof tiles rained down.

The Tang family manor was built against a high cliff.

Su Yi-shu, a White-rank martial artist from Desolate Fortress's Sichuan branch, clung like a spider to one side of the cliff overlooking the manor, cold sweat running down his skin.

Though he had cultivated the Gecko Technique well enough to maintain this position for a day and a half without strain, his very soul seemed to flee at the scale of the battlefield below.

Blade-like surging auras. The aftermath of foot energy that pressed faintly against the skin before rising.

There were even supreme masters moving at such staggering speeds that their bodies appeared translucent as they traversed the manor.

Su Yi-shu swallowed hard. Sweat formed on the palms that channeled his true energy into the wall.

'Ten Perfections Sect, Pure Demon Alliance, Yu Ling, and even the subordinate sects that have surrendered to them... this is insane. The branch leader should have come personally...'

Though sporadic battles had been erupting across the martial world recently, it was another matter entirely for a wing of the Eight Great Clans to be attacked on such a massive scale.

This wasn't merely a fight between martial sects. Especially since it involved the Tang family.

The Sichuan Tang family was one of the few among the Eight Great Clans with good relations with Desolate Fortress, the reason being their role in maintaining order in Sichuan's martial world.

A major faction that possessed great power yet didn't engage in exploitation, cooperating appropriately with Desolate Fortress.

Such places as the Tang family were exceedingly rare among the martial sects. Especially in Sichuan.

For Desolate Fortress, perpetually shorthanded, it was like sweet dew.

They absolutely must not fall — yet they seemed destined for annihilation. That was how it appeared to Su Yi-shu.

"Go in! It's safe to enter!"

"The poison mist has been inhaled by those dead ones! No need to circulate energy unnecessarily!"

The main gate of the Tang family.

People entering by stepping over corpses with blue-tinged faces.

The Nine Poison Formation, said to transform the entrance of the Tang family into the netherworld itself, had long since exhausted its power after turning the martial artists of the Ten Perfections Sect's subordinate sects into wandering ghosts.

The path leading to the wide front yard was the same.

The mechanism formation of Silent Dragon Seeking Fear, composed of bundles of poison-coated long needles, had activated from beneath the ground and skewered dozens, yet the Sima Evil Path martial artists trampled over the corpses like shields and moved through the Tang family manor as if it were their own home.

The most striking sight lay elsewhere.

[Even after decades of friendship, is the Tang Clan Leader still merely preserving his seat!]

An old man with a flowing white beard like an immortal.

Wearing dark green gloves glistening with poison, he didn't spare the surrounding Tang family martial artists so much as a glance, simply standing in place and occasionally flicking his fingers.

Each time, black smoke was etched in streams across the void. Finger energy.

The greenish mist generated by the Tang family's Ghost King Spirit poison formation would burn and vanish, and occasionally the bodies of Tang family martial artists caught in those strikes would burst apart.

The realm of wielding tremendous power at will. A sight beyond the mortal world.

The new Pure Demon Alliance Leader.

The teacher of the previous Pure Demon Alliance Leader, who had met her death after a thousand-exchange battle with Ming Cult's Young Sect Leader.

Originally, he had retired from the front lines as an elder council leader due to the side effects of demonic arts, but rumors were widespread that he had reached the realm of demonic transcendence upon hearing of his disciple's death.

'That old monster just won't die. Even he alone is a calamity upon the mortal world...'

Su Yi-shu carefully shifted his gaze. There was another figure of particular concern below.

A man wearing thin white armor.

Like the new Pure Demon Alliance Leader, he was surrounded by Tang family martial artists, but at first glance, he didn't appear to have cultivated martial arts at all — his energy was utterly calm. Yet he was a peerless master who had refined more weapons to their extreme than anyone else in the world.

'Ten Perfections Sect Leader...!'

Left Sword Right Blade. Each time he thrust into the void with the sword in his left hand and swept the great blade in his right, the air undulated violently.

The foot energy generated by his weapons was powerful enough to cause earthquakes in space.

Eventually, it proved sufficient to shatter the bodies of dozens of Tang family martial artists who had surrounded themselves with blue lightning using the Heaven Thunder Rainstorm Formation.

Kwakwakwakwakwang—!

The Taoists of Qingcheng Sect and the monks of Emei Sect retreated, hesitant. A dramatic shift in the front line that had held for days with the support of the two Nine Sects.

'The Tang family won't be annihilated immediately.'

Su Yi-shu thought.

For some reason, Sichuan's Sima Evil Path kept slowly strangling the Tang family's breath.

As if waiting for something. Otherwise, the Tang family would have been destroyed long ago.

'Still, I should report immediately. How many formations have been broken...'

Tuk.

He tried not to make a sound as he pressed against the cliff wall.

"What are you?"

"Hup...!"

In that instant, Su Yi-shu's breath stopped. His body froze in the Gecko Technique posture, clinging to the wall like a spider.

A cool sensation spread across the nape of his neck. He could barely move his eyeballs.

An old man standing on the void behind him.

His snow-white beard was neatly arranged down past his sternum. Pure black sparks continuously danced over his navy blue long robe, which was decorated with elaborate landscape paintings.

That appearance suited the atmosphere of the old man, who was smiling brightly.

"Fairly well-refined energy, absurd espionage... are you one of Desolate Fortress's lackeys?"

The old Pure Demon Alliance Leader asked slowly. Black flames like abyssal fire blazed in both eyes, giving the impression that he wasn't particularly bothering to conceal his energy.

Su Yi-shu couldn't answer.

He merely trembled as the cold sweat on his nape turned to ice. The presence pressing against his skin was immense.

An opponent impossible to face. That was what it meant to be a peerless master.

Su Yi-shu inwardly cursed Zhang Ri-dao, the Desolate Fortress Qizhou branch leader, without pause.

No matter how many earth-shattering events had occurred, did you really have to send me to such a place?

The old Pure Demon Alliance Leader smiled.

His low voice was that of the Yama King himself.

It was then.

Creeak—

The small sound of wheels rolling was unusually clear.

Near the distant main gate of the Tang family.

A young man wearing an antique black robe slowly crossed the threshold, pulling a very shabby cart behind him.

[TL Note- Hope JYS brought some extra caskets for the fools those are about to die.]
